title: Academic General Education Requirements
content:
Morgan State University General Education Requirements for SCMNS and Computer Science advising
Source: SCMNS GenEds.pdf, 2024-2026 Undergraduate Catalog printout.

The General Education Program distribution areas total 40 credits. University Requirements add 2 credits. These requirements are part of degree progress and should be considered with CS major, supporting, elective, and prerequisite planning.

Information, Technological and Media Literacy (IM), 3 credits: complete one option. Listed options: COSC 110 - Introduction to Computing; INSS 141 - Digital Literacy and Application Software; or the computer literacy course required by the major/discipline. CS majors may satisfy computing literacy through the CS curriculum when the official degree plan indicates it.

English Composition (EC), 6 credits: complete one Part A course and one Part B course with required sequence grades. Part A: ENGL 101 - Composition I; ENGL 111 - Composition I Honors. Part B: ENGL 102 - Composition II; ENGL 112 - Composition II Honors. ENGL 101 is prerequisite for ENGL 102; ENGL 111 is prerequisite for ENGL 112.

Critical Thinking (CT), 3 credits: complete one option. Listed options: ARCH 105; COMM 300; ENGR 110; MHTC 340; PHIL 109; PHIL 119.

Mathematics and Quantitative Reasoning (MQ), 3 credits required; must be MATH 109 or above. Listed options: MATH 109; MATH 110; MATH 113; or MQ course required by the major/discipline. CS majors typically satisfy MQ through MATH 241 - Calculus I.

Arts and Humanities (AH), 6 credits: complete two courses from different disciplines. Listed options: ART 308; COMM 203; ENGL 347; HUMA 201; HUMA 211; HUMA 202; HUMA 212; HUMA 301; MISC 302; MUSC 391; PHEC 300; PHIL 102; PHIL 220; PHIL 223; RELG 305; THEA 210; THEA 312; Foreign Language 102 or higher; WGST 301.

Biological and Physical Sciences (BP), 7 credits: complete two courses; at least one must be lab-based. Lab-based listed options: BIOL 101, BIOL 102, BIOL 103, BIOL 109, BIOL 110, BIOL 111, BIOL 112, CHEM 101 with CHEM 101L, CHEM 105 with CHEM 105L, CHEM 110 with CHEM 110L, CHEM 111 with CHEM 111L, CHEM 112 with CHEM 112L, EASC 205, PHYS 101, PHYS 111, PHYS 203 with PHYS 203L, PHYS 205 with PHYS 205L, PHYS 206 with PHYS 206L. Non-lab listed options: EASC 101; EASC 102; EASC 201; EASC 202; EASC 203; EASC 301; GEOG 101; GEOG 104; GEOG 105; PHYS 105; PHYS 310; PHYS 311; PHYS 408; TRSS 301.

Social and Behavioral Sciences (SB), 6 credits: complete two courses from different disciplines. Listed options: ECON 211; ECON 212; HIST 101; HIST 102; HIST 105; HIST 106; HIST 111; HIST 112; HIST 115; HIST 116; HIST 120; HIST 130; HIST 140; MHTC 103; MISC 301; POSC 201; POSC 206; PSYC 101; PSYC 111; SOCI 101; SOCI 110; SOSC 101.

Health and Healthful Living (HH), 3 credits: complete one option. Listed options: HLTH 103; HLTH 200; HLTH 203; HLTH 300; NUSC 160.

Contemporary and Global Issues, Ideas and Values (CI), 3 credits: complete one option. Listed options: AFST 350; HIST 350; HIST 360; WGST 201.

University Requirements, 2 credits: complete one activity/financial literacy option and one school orientation option. Activity/financial literacy options: any PHEC activity course, FIN 101, MIND 101, or DSVG 101. Orientation options include ORLA 101, ORBM 102, ORED 103, OREN 104, ORNS 106, ORAP 107, ORCH 109, ORSW 101, ORSJ 101, HONR 101, and HONR 106. For SCMNS students, ORNS 106 is the standard orientation option; HONR 106 is the Freshman Honors in SCMNS option.

Advising guidance: General Education options are valid recommendations when they satisfy outstanding requirements, balance workload, or keep a student progressing while CS prerequisites are in progress. Completed and in-progress courses count for planning checks; in-progress prerequisite use should be described as conditional on successful completion. Avoid duplicate credit assumptions: a course may satisfy both a major/supporting requirement and a General Education area, but credits count only once toward the total degree credits.
